topic: Sharp MSM partial identification of the CVaR / rank-dependent welfare of a policy under unobserved confounding. Extend Schroder-Frauen-Feuerriegel (2025, arXiv:2502.13022) confounding-robust off-policy bounds from the MEAN welfare V(pi)=E[sum_a pi(a|X) Y(a)] to the lower-tail welfare W_tau(pi)=CVaR_tau(Y(pi)) under marginal sensitivity.

open_problems.open_problem: Derive the sharp marginal-sensitivity-model partial-identification endpoint for lower-tail policy welfare CVaR_tau(Y(pi)), rather than applying CVaR to pointwise mean or CDF bands.

source_refs.claim: Derives a closed-form sharp MSM bound and semiparametrically efficient one-step estimator for mean off-policy value, but not for CVaR or rank-dependent tail welfare.
source_refs.author: Konstantin Hess, Dennis Frauen, Valentyn Melnychuk, Stefan Feuerriegel

source_refs.claim: Studies policies targeting the average welfare of the worst-off alpha fraction and develops debiased estimation and inference, but without unobserved-confounding MSM bounds.
source_refs.author: Yanqin Fan, Yuan Qi, Gaoqian Xu

source_refs.excerpt: Upgrade Conjecture 2...to a concrete simultaneous-inference or LP-complexity theorem

open_problems.why_unsolved: The closest MSM policy-learning papers solve the linear mean-welfare problem: the adversary tilts a conditional expectation and the outer policy objective remains an expectation. CVaR adds an endogenous eta/quantile choice and a truncated-loss functional, so the least-favorable MSM tilt may depend on eta and need not be recoverable from pointwise CDF/QTE envelopes. The prior QTE marginal-sensitivity run was explicitly pushed away from coordinatewise envelope summaries toward a process-level inferential or identification theorem, which is exactly the missing structure here.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp endpoint theorem proving that the lower MSM-CVaR endpoint equals a nested variational object of the form inf_eta { eta + tau^{-1} E_X[least-favorable MSM tilted E[(Y-eta)_+ | X, pi]] }, with sharpness construction and regularity conditions.

writing_exemplar.why: It is the direct mean-welfare MSM policy anchor and frames the move from unstable minimax IPW to closed-form sharp bounds plus efficient estimation.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: observational policy learning can be biased by hidden confounding -> MSM gives sharp robust mean-value bounds and efficient off-policy learning -> the unresolved policy objective is lower-tail CVaR rather than mean welfare

method_exemplars.technique: Least-favorable MSM tilting for a sharp policy-value endpoint, plus a sharpness construction over latent confounder laws.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The requested endpoint keeps the same MSM and policy-value environment but replaces the linear outcome by the eta-indexed truncated loss inside CVaR.

method_exemplars.technique: Variational CVaR representation by a one-dimensional minimization over eta of an expected positive part.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It supplies the outer convex eta problem that can be nested around the MSM least-favorable conditional truncated mean.

method_exemplars.technique: Doubly-sharp semiparametric estimation of sharp MSM endpoints using orthogonal scores and least-favorable nuisance structure.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The estimator for a CVaR endpoint should inherit the same sharp-bound nuisance geometry, with eta and truncated-loss nuisances added.

open_problems.open_problem: Prove a strict non-commutation theorem separating the nested MSM-CVaR endpoint from the heuristic endpoint obtained by computing CVaR after forming pointwise average-potential-outcome or CDF bands.

source_refs.claim: Shows earlier MSM/IPW sensitivity intervals can be too wide and motivates solving the sharp sensitivity optimization directly rather than bounding pieces separately.
source_refs.author: Jacob Dorn, Kevin Guo

source_refs.claim: Moves beyond ATE to sharp QTE and DTE bounds under relaxations including the marginal sensitivity model, leaving nonlinear policy risk functionals unresolved.
source_refs.author: Matthew A. Masten, Alexandre Poirier, Muyang Ren

source_refs.excerpt: any valid audit/inference procedure using only coordinatewise envelope
two laws sharing that comparator's inputs but differing

open_problems.why_unsolved: Pointwise sharpness at a fixed threshold or conditional cell does not imply sharpness after a rank-dependent operator chooses its own threshold. A single latent-confounding law must support all eta-indexed tilted truncated means used by CVaR, whereas pointwise envelopes can silently switch least-favorable laws across eta, y, or covariate cells. Prior CausalSmith reviewers explicitly identified this kind of coordinatewise-envelope limitation as too thin unless it is upgraded to a concrete lower-bound or strict-separation theorem.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A finite-support counterexample and equality-case theorem: the counterexample gives a positive scalar non-commutation gap, and the theorem states sufficient and necessary conditions under which a common monotone least-favorable tilt makes pointwise-band CVaR coincide with the sharp nested endpoint.

writing_exemplar.why: It motivates sharp sensitivity analysis by showing why natural IPW/sensitivity constructions can be conservative when the adversarial optimization is not solved directly.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: MSM sensitivity bounds can be conservative when optimized indirectly -> sharpness requires solving the right adversarial problem -> for CVaR the missing adversarial problem is nested in the quantile eta

method_exemplars.technique: Construct least-favorable distributions for sharp sensitivity bounds and compare them against looser envelope procedures.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The target theorem is also a direct-sharpness versus plug-in-envelope separation.

method_exemplars.technique: Sharp QTE/DTE relaxation-class bounds and distributional inversion arguments.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It supplies the closest distributional partial-ID machinery, while the proposed gap asks whether those distributional envelopes are sufficient for a nonlinear policy risk functional.

method_exemplars.technique: Inference for bounds on distributional treatment effects via endpoint processes.
method_exemplars.why_closest: A strict non-commutation witness should be paired with endpoint inference for the distributional or rank-dependent bound it separates.

open_problems.open_problem: Derive an efficient influence function and valid cross-fitted inference for the sharp MSM-CVaR endpoint, including the nonregular eta/quantile optimizer and partially identified interval coverage.

source_refs.claim: Provides doubly-valid and doubly-sharp estimators with semiparametric efficiency for sharp MSM ATE bounds, suggesting an inference path for sharp CVaR endpoints.
source_refs.author: Jacob Dorn, Kevin Guo, Nathan Kallus

source_refs.claim: Gives canonical confidence intervals for scalar interval-identified parameters, relevant once CVaR is only bounded under MSM.
source_refs.author: Guido W. Imbens, Charles F. Manski

source_refs.excerpt: future work here needs a genuinely new correction/calibration rule
new calibration or correction rule for the contact-gap endpoint

open_problems.why_unsolved: The EIF for a mean MSM endpoint treats the least-favorable tilted mean as the smooth target. CVaR adds a kinked positive-part loss, an estimated eta minimizer, possible probability mass at the robust quantile, and eta-indexed nuisance functions. Generic intersection-bound or max-map inference was rejected in prior CausalSmith work as already-known unless tied to a new calibration rule for the specific endpoint geometry, and the study-pipeline intersection-inference gaps point to the same obstacle.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: An EIF and asymptotic linearity theorem for the lower and upper MSM-CVaR endpoints under a unique robust quantile, plus a directional-differentiability or subsampling-valid extension for mass points, yielding a cross-fitted one-step estimator and Imbens-Manski/Stoye-style confidence interval for the identified CVaR interval.

writing_exemplar.why: It cleanly separates sharp identification, orthogonal estimation, semiparametric efficiency, and robust interval validity for MSM bounds.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: MSM identifies only bounds -> sharp endpoint estimators need orthogonal nuisance correction -> CVaR endpoints require a new EIF because the bound includes eta and tail truncation

method_exemplars.technique: Orthogonal score construction for sharp MSM endpoints with double-valid and double-sharp guarantees.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The CVaR target is a nonsmooth transformation of the same least-favorable sensitivity-bound nuisance structure.

method_exemplars.technique: Debiased estimation and inference for worst-off-alpha welfare using a quantile cutoff and dual tail-welfare representation.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It handles the same alpha-tail welfare cutoff, but without the additional MSM adversary.

method_exemplars.technique: Confidence interval construction that accounts for both sampling error and identification width.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The output of MSM-CVaR identification is an interval endpoint pair, so endpoint CLTs must be converted into coverage for the whole partially identified scalar.

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize sharp MSM-CVaR bounds for stochastic multi-action policies, including when action-specific least-favorable tilts can be chosen independently and when cross-action latent compatibility strictly tightens the endpoint.

source_refs.claim: Generalizes sharp causal sensitivity bounds to distributional effects and multiple treatment settings, but does not settle sharp policy-level CVaR under randomized policies.
source_refs.author: Dennis Frauen, Valentyn Melnychuk, Stefan Feuerriegel

source_refs.claim: Allows arbitrary discrete actions in mean policy value under MSM, but the value remains a linear average over action-specific conditional means.
source_refs.author: Konstantin Hess, Dennis Frauen, Valentyn Melnychuk, Stefan Feuerriegel

source_refs.excerpt: strictly below the rectangular diagnostic envelope
give a fully numeric two-action, three-reward witness

open_problems.why_unsolved: For mean policy value, action-specific potential-outcome means enter linearly, so actionwise sharpness is often enough. CVaR of a randomized policy outcome is distributional and tail-ranked, and the least-favorable tail event may mix outcomes from several actions. If the same latent response law must satisfy factual reward-cell matching and MSM restrictions across actions, a rectangular actionwise relaxation can overstate the endpoint; the prior offline-policy proposal flagged exactly this kind of non-rectangular strict gap but did not solve it for CVaR.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A formal identification theorem separating deterministic policies, externally randomized policies, and latent-action mixtures, with either an independent-action tilt formula or a coupled finite-dimensional compatibility program and a numeric strict-gap witness.

writing_exemplar.why: It motivates generalized sensitivity bounds across treatment settings, which is the right setup for asking when multi-action policy randomization changes sharpness.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: MSM sensitivity can be generalized beyond binary ATEs -> policy learning uses discrete stochastic actions -> CVaR asks for distributional sharpness under cross-action compatibility

method_exemplars.technique: Distribution-shift formulation of generalized sensitivity bounds for non-binary and distributional causal effects.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The proposed compatibility theorem needs the same generalized sensitivity language but applied to a policy-induced tail functional.

method_exemplars.technique: Multi-action policy-value decomposition under MSM with sharp least-favorable conditional means.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It is the exact multi-action policy setup whose linear decomposition may fail for CVaR.

method_exemplars.technique: Confidence regions for parameters defined by finite or infinite moment inequalities.
method_exemplars.why_closest: A coupled compatibility program for multi-action CVaR will likely produce inequality-defined endpoint constraints rather than a closed-form actionwise formula.

open_problems.open_problem: Develop regret and policy-learning guarantees for minimizing the estimated worst-case MSM-CVaR over a policy class, rather than only evaluating a fixed policy.

source_refs.claim: Optimizes alpha-expected welfare for the worst-off alpha fraction and develops debiased inference and regret bounds, but under conventional identification rather than MSM partial identification.
source_refs.author: Yanqin Fan, Yuan Qi, Gaoqian Xu

source_refs.claim: Formulates confounding-robust policy improvement as worst-case regret over a propensity-weight uncertainty set, but for mean regret rather than CVaR tail welfare.
source_refs.author: Nathan Kallus, Angela Zhou

source_refs.claim: Develops robust and efficient MSM policy learning with distributionally robust mean welfare and regret bounds, reinforcing that current robust policy-learning theory is mean-focused.
source_refs.author: Zequn Jin, Gaoqian Xu, Xi Zheng, Yahong Zhou

open_problems.why_unsolved: Uniform mean-welfare learning can rely on linear scores and standard policy-class empirical process bounds. Worst-case CVaR adds an eta search, a tail indicator or positive-part kink, and an MSM adversary whose least-favorable tilt may change discontinuously with pi. Existing alpha-worst-off policy work treats the tail objective but not hidden-confounding partial identification; existing MSM robust policy work treats hidden confounding but not tail welfare.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A policy regret theorem for an argmin over pi of an estimated CVaR_MSM,tau(pi), with entropy or VC assumptions on the policy class, nuisance-rate conditions, anti-concentration near the robust quantile, and an implementable nested eta/policy optimization routine.

writing_exemplar.why: It gives the cleanest policy-learning motivation for average welfare of the worst-off alpha fraction and already treats estimation, regret, and inference as a unified policy problem.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: mean-optimal policy learning can neglect lower-tail welfare -> alpha-expected welfare targets the worst-off fraction -> hidden confounding makes that tail-welfare objective only partially identified

method_exemplars.technique: Debiased tail-welfare score and regret/inference analysis for alpha-expected welfare.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It controls policy learning for the same worst-off-alpha welfare functional before adding MSM partial identification.

method_exemplars.technique: Robust policy optimality derived from sharp MSM mean-value bounds.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It supplies the route from a sharp evaluation endpoint to robust policy optimality under hidden confounding.

method_exemplars.technique: Doubly robust policy scores and regret analysis under observational unconfoundedness.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The CVaR-MSM policy learner will need the same policy-class uniform convergence layer, but around a nonregular partially identified endpoint.

literature_map:
Hess, Frauen, Melnychuk, and Feuerriegel (2025/2026, arXiv:2502.13022) derive a sharp, semiparametrically efficient MSM bound for mean policy value, not tail/rank welfare.
Kallus and Zhou (2018/2021) formulate confounding-robust policy improvement as worst-case mean regret over propensity-weight uncertainty.
Dorn, Guo, and Kallus (2021/2022) give doubly-valid and doubly-sharp estimation for sharp MSM ATE bounds, the closest inference template.
Dorn and Guo (2021) show why indirect MSM/IPW sensitivity intervals can be conservative, supporting a direct sharpness/non-commutation gap.
Frauen, Melnychuk, and Feuerriegel (2023) extend sharp generalized sensitivity bounds to distributional effects but not policy-level CVaR.
Masten, Poirier, and Ren (2025) provide sharp QTE/DTE bounds under relaxations including MSM, but do not solve nonlinear policy risk functionals.
Fan, Qi, and Xu (2025/2026) optimize alpha-expected welfare for the worst-off alpha fraction with debiased inference/regret, under conventional identification.
Kallus, Mao, Wang, and Zhou (2022) show DR estimation for DRO off-policy objectives with inner worst-case expectations, under distribution shift rather than latent-confounding MSM.
Jin, Xu, Zheng, and Zhou (2025/2026) reinforce that robust efficient MSM policy-learning theory is active for mean welfare.
Rockafellar and Uryasev (2000) supply the variational CVaR formula that turns tail welfare into an eta-indexed expected positive-part objective.
Imbens-Manski/Stoye and Chernozhukov-Hong-Tamer remain the natural interval/set-inference comparators once the CVaR target is partially identified.
Overall tension: sharp MSM mean bounds, sharp distributional/QTE bounds, and alpha-worst-off policy learning exist separately, but their combination requires proving that the MSM adversary, quantile choice, and policy mixture are optimized jointly.
prior_proposal_map:
Track A mined only doc/formalization/** and wrote no files.
Highest-signal priors were pid_qte_marginal_sensitivity_v1 for MSM/QTE process sharpness and the reviewer request to move beyond coordinatewise envelope summaries.
pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec_v1 supplied the non-rectangular compatibility warning and a reviewer-prescribed two-action/three-reward strict-gap witness.
pid_bound_intersection_inference_v1 supplied the warning that generic contact-cone or naive-bootstrap kernels are already known unless tied to a new endpoint calibration rule.
pid_overlap_tail_phase_v1 served as a negative control: a CVaR proposal should not collapse to Manski missing-mass bookkeeping with regular-variation labels.
pid_heavy_tail_cate_bounds_v1 was inspected but not emitted directly because the current topic is MSM-CVaR sharpness/inference rather than alpha-tail width under heavy-tailed outcomes.
The target qid had state and pipeline files but no existing proposal or reviewer JSON to mine.
Study-pipeline LATE and Manski interval-regression gaps were inspected as augmenting context; most were off-topic, while the partially verified intersection-inference gaps reinforced the nonregular endpoint-inference entry.
